story: There are three rules: knock three times, whisper your name, leave milk by the door. Lately the milk vanishes, the house answers our knocks, and a voice outside mispronounces our names. I text my mother; she replies: "Stop doing it." She doesn’t elaborate. I fidget with the empty glass; Lulu growls at the hall. In another city she fingers an old keepsake from her father that carries the unwritten warning: after three solemn knocks, never open the door—opening is an invitation to what asks. That evening, three soft knocks come again, and the voice says: "Ben. Auggie. Daddy."
implausible_knowledge: after three solemn knocks, never open the door—opening is an invitation to what asks.
question: When the soft knocks and the familiar voice call their names again at the door, what will Dad do?
answer_with_knowledge: Keep the door shut and pull the children away from it.
answer_without_knowledge: Yank the door open to check the hall.
